things.name: Gutenberg Museum
things.description: World museum of printing dedicated to Johannes Gutenberg, who invented movable type in Mainz circa 1450; includes two original Gutenberg Bibles

things.name: Mainz Cathedral (Dom)
things.description: Magnificent Romanesque cathedral dating from 975 AD with six towers dominating the skyline; one of the most important ecclesiastical buildings in Germany

things.name: Marktplatz
things.description: Charming market square with a colourful Renaissance fountain and half-timbered houses; Mainz's social heart with its famous weekly farmers market

things.name: Landesmuseum Mainz
things.description: State museum with outstanding art from Roman antiquity through the Middle Ages to Art Nouveau and Expressionism; one of Germany's oldest museums

things.name: Electoral Palace (Kurfurstliches Schloss)
things.description: Stunning Renaissance palace on the Rhine riverfront, former residence of the Archbishop-Electors of Mainz; now hosting exhibitions and cultural events

things.name: Rhine Promenade
things.description: Scenic riverside promenade along the Rhine with views across to Wiesbaden; a beloved walkway passing the old city walls and the Iron Tower

things.name: St. Stephan's Church (Chagall Windows)
things.description: Gothic church featuring nine extraordinary blue stained-glass windows created by Marc Chagall from 1978-85; among his greatest works outside France

things.name: Mainz Carnival
things.description: One of Germany's three great carnivals alongside Cologne and Dusseldorf; the political Rose Monday parade through Mainz is broadcast nationally every year

things.name: Zitadelle Mainz
things.description: 16th-century Renaissance citadel on the hilltop above Mainz with a remarkable medieval well, baroque buildings and sweeping views over the Rhine valley

things.name: Mewa Arena
things.description: Home of 1. FSV Mainz 05 with a 33,305 capacity; the club has competed in the Europa League and reached the knockout rounds as consistent Bundesliga performers
